name: Milhombres Trail
description: This difficult 38.2-mile mountain biking route, Ruta Milhombres — circular desde Luna, offers challenging terrain and scenic views near Luna
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Luna traverse a varied landscape, featuring rolling hills and more challenging ascents. The region offers diverse terrain suitable for different skill levels, from gentle paths to demanding routes with significant elevation changes. Riders can expect a mix of open areas and possibly some wooded sections, providing a range of riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Luna The most popular mountain bike trail is Milhombres Trail, a 38.2 miles (61.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route features nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Aguarales de Valdemilaz — circular desde Piedratajada por Valpalmas, a moderate 24.6 miles (39.6 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain with over 470 meters of climbing. Local mountain bikers also love the Piedratajada - Salto del Lobo Loop, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Luna is defined by rolling hills, open landscapes, and some challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Luna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 210 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Luna?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Luna, offering a diverse range of experiences for riders. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Luna?
A: Yes, Luna offers some easier options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987199379/piedratajada-salto-del-lobo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada - Salto del Lobo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.7-mile trail that can be completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes, leading through varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Luna?
A: Mountain biking around Luna is characterized by rolling hills, open landscapes, and some challenging climbs. The region features varied terrain, from gentle paths to demanding routes with significant elevation changes, providing a range of riding experiences.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Luna has several difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986879763/milhombres-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Milhombres Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.2-mile route with nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain, typically taking over 5 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of La Mula – Pino de Val de Navarro loop from Castejón de Valdejasa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 59.2 miles with over 1,100 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Luna?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Luna are circular. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759893/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-circular-desde-piedratajada-por-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz — circular desde Piedratajada por Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.6-mile path with over 470 meters of climbing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987199379/piedratajada-salto-del-lobo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada - Salto del Lobo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another circular trail.

Q: What are some scenic views or landmarks I can see along the trails?
A: The trails around Luna offer opportunities to see spectacular scenic landscapes. You can also explore natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt; or the unique formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of La Mula&lt;/a&gt; also suggests panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in Luna?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the easier routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987199379/piedratajada-salto-del-lobo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedratajada - Salto del Lobo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.7 miles and relatively flat, could be suitable for families with some biking experience. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Luna?
A: The mountain bike trails in Luna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. Riders often praise the varied landscapes, the mix of open areas and challenging ascents, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Is Luna a good destination for long-distance mountain biking?
A: Yes, Luna offers several long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986879763/milhombres-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Milhombres Trail&lt;/a&gt; is over 38 miles long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of La Mula – Pino de Val de Navarro loop&lt;/a&gt; extends to nearly 60 miles, providing excellent options for endurance riders.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Luna well-maintained?
A: While specific maintenance details are not provided, the high average rating of 4.5 stars from the komoot community suggests that riders generally have positive experiences with the trails, implying they are in good condition for mountain biking.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find cultural and historical points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María de la Corona in Ejea de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building in the vicinity, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt; is another man-made highlight.
tips: Viewpoint that we find on the ascent to Punta de San Jorge with good views of Monlore and on clear days of Moncayo and the western peaks of the Pyrenees

Located at an altitude of 550 meters and located in the Cinco Villas region, we find this deserted area that was made up of 14 houses, a church and two ovens for making bread and presses for making wine.

Obano Castle is an Aragonese fortification built in the second half of the 11th century, during the reign of Sancho Ramírez. It is located in the municipality of Luna, in the Cinco Villas region. This Romanesque-style castle was built to guard the communication routes between Huesca and Ejea de los Caballeros.

The castle is located near the Arba de Biel River and is known for its defensive tower, which rises to approximately 16 meters in height. Throughout its history, the castle has belonged to the Knights Templar and the Monastery of San Juan de la Peña.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Milhombres — circular desde Luna suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging mountain biking trail rated as 'difficult' by komoot. It requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Some sections have a technical difficulty rating of S3 to S6, meaning you might encounter large obstacles, steep gradients, and even need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ruta Milhombres?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, with sections ranging from S3 to S6 on the Single Track Scale. Expect technical passages with roots, rocks, steep gradients, and tight turns. Higher-rated sections (S4-S6) may feature very steep, exposed areas and loose surfaces, requiring expert riding technique.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Ruta Milhombres — circular desde Luna?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 61.4 kilometers long and typically takes around 5 hours and 36 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can discover several interesting points. Keep an eye out for the Viewpoint of Punta de San Jorge, the historical Abandoned village of Júnez, and the impressive Castillo de Obano. The trail also offers picturesque views of the surrounding mountains and natural landscapes characteristic of the Luna area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta Milhombres — circular desde Luna?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Luna, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but local street parking or designated public parking lots in Luna would be your best bet.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Milhombres — circular desde Luna dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but given its challenging nature and length, it's only suitable for very fit dogs accustomed to long, strenuous rides. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details for this route are not provided, mountain biking in this region of Spain is generally best during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer milder temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or colder conditions in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Ruta Milhombres?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for the Ruta Milhombres itself. However, as the trail is located in the Cinco Villas region, it's always good practice to be aware of any general regulations or protected area guidelines that might apply. Typically, public trails in Spain do not require individual permits.

faqs.question: Does the Ruta Milhombres — circular desde Luna intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official trails. You will encounter parts of Val del Rey, Val de Rompesacos, PR-Z 104, Sierra Mayor a Luna, Cañada de Paul de Montanal, and PR-Z 101 along the way.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes/patches), a first-aid kit, appropriate clothing layers, sun protection, and a fully charged phone with the komoot app for navigation. A helmet is essential, and protective gear like knee and elbow pads is highly recommended due to the technical terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the Ruta Milhombres trailhead in Luna?
faqs.answer: Luna, being the starting and ending point of this circular route, offers basic amenities. You should be able to find local cafes or pubs in the town where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially if you plan an early start or late finish.
